questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A string of negligible mass, natural length $L$ has Young's modulus $Y$. The string hangs from roof with masses $m_1$ and $m_2$ as shown in the figure. If mass $m_2$ is removed gently, the mass $m_1$ is just able to bounce back upto point $O$. Find the ratio $\mathrm{m}_2 / \mathrm{m}_1$. (string will not obstruct the motion of mass $\mathrm{m}_1$ and system is initially in equilibrium).
questions.solution:
Using condition of equilibrium
$\mathrm{KL}=\left(\mathrm{m}_1+\mathrm{m}_2\right) \mathrm{g}$
Using COME
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{KL}^2=\mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{~g}(2 \mathrm{~L}) \\& \Rightarrow \mathrm{m}_1=\frac{\mathrm{KL}}{4 \mathrm{~g}}=\frac{\left(\mathrm{m}_1+\mathrm{m}_2\right)}{4} \\& \Rightarrow 3 \mathrm{~m}_1=\mathrm{m}_2=\mathrm{m}_2 / \mathrm{m}_1=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: In the adjacent figure, ABCD are four points in a smooth horizontal plane representing the four corners of a square of diagonal 20 cm . A very small object of mass 14 gm placed at the centre of the square connected to points A, B, C and D by four elastic strings of same dimensions but of different moduli of elasticity as shown in the figure. Natural length and cross-sectional area of each string are 10 cm and $\pi^2 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}^2$ respectively. The mass is slightly displaced from equilibrium and then released to perform SHM along the diagonal AC. $\left(Y_1=9 \mathrm{Y}, \mathrm{Y}_2=4 \mathrm{Y}, \mathrm{Y}_3=16 \mathrm{Y}\right.$ and $\mathrm{Y}_4=$ 4 Y , where $\mathrm{Y}=4.9 \times 10^9 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$ ). The time period of oscillation of object is k millisecond. The value of K is

Masses of 20 kg and 10 kg hang from them as shown in the figure. The maximum mass that can be placed in the pan without breaking the wires is _____kg (Use g=10 m s-2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The equation of a longitudinal stationary wave in a metal rod is given by, $y=0.002 \sin \frac{\pi x}{3}$. $\sin 1000 \pi \mathrm{t}$ where x and y are in cm and t is in seconds. Maximum change in pressure (the maximum tensile stress) at the point $x=2 \mathrm{~m}$, (if young's modulus of the material is $\frac{3}{8 \pi} \mathrm{dynes} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ ) is $\frac{1}{p} \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{dy} / \mathrm{cm}^2$. Find p
questions.solution:
Maximum change in pressure
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Maximum tensile stress }=\text { YAK } \\
& =\frac{3}{8 \pi} \times 10^{-3} \times \frac{\pi}{3} \\
& =\frac{1}{8} \times 10^{-3} \text { dyne } / \mathrm{cm}^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A coil carrying a current $I=10 \mathrm{~mA}$ is placed in a uniform magnetic field so that its axis coincides with the field direction. The coil consist of only one turn and made up of copper. The diameter of the wire is 0.1 mm , the radius of the coil is $R=3 \pi \mathrm{cm}$. The value of external induction field will the coil rupture is 500 K . Breaking stress of copper $=3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$. Then find the value of $K$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
